claims.summary: Solo and joint phase transitions need durable cards with ownership, parent anchors, vectors, duration, phenomenology, reply state, and replay identity.
claims.disposition: verify current phase-transition card, affordance, witness, reply, and persistence behavior

claims.summary: Transition shape should include entropy, gradient, dispersal, and any grounded magnetization evidence rather than fill delta alone.
claims.disposition: verify existing entropy, density-gradient, dispersal, and vector fields; route magnetization as bounded non-live evidence work because no canonical field currently exists

claims.summary: Joint-transition comparison across correspondence and journals must remain descriptive and must not unlock behavior or control.
claims.disposition: verify joint-room and correspondence anchors and preserve operator approval for any runtime effect
